title: thesium refractum var. saxatile
intro:
Thesium refractum var. saxatile, also known as the Alpine toothwort, is a flowering plant species within the Thesium genus, characterized by its unique, toothed leaves and ability to thrive in alpine regions.

This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits associated with Thesium refractum var. saxatile include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for herbal use through infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are often employed to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
